title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: UNINTENTIONAL DEPLOYMENT OF AIRBAG
Defect: A possible fault in the microprocessor in the air bag control unit could lead to an uncontrolled setting of the output signals from the control unit immediately after switching on the ignition. In unfavourable conditions this may lead to components of the safety and restraint system being triggered. This fault does not affect the vehicle while the car is being driven.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace and code the air bag control unit.

title: 5 SERIES

Concern: THE PRESSING FORCE OF THE BEARING BUSHING INTO THE CRANKCASE MAY BE OUT OF SPECIFICATION
Defect: The engine counterbalance shaft bearing bushing into the crankcase may be out of specification
Remedy: Replace the engine of affected vehicles

Remedy: Install a modified rubber spacer and replace any brake lines showing signs of chafing.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: AN ADDITIONAL CRASH PAD IS REQUIRED BEHIND THE AUXILIARY BATTERY
Defect: To comply with the regulation an additional crash pad is required behind the auxiliary battery.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the crash pad will be retrofitted.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: EPS MAY NOT PERFORM AS SPECIFIED
Defect: Due to a component in the integrated control module not being to specification it is possible power steering assistance may fail. If this happens the EPS warning lamp will illuminate and a check control message will appear on the dashboard display. Directional control will be maintained albeit with additional driver input.
Remedy: Recall vehicles likely to be affected to replace the steering box.

Concern: THE DAMPING ELEMENTS MAY BE MISSING FROM SOME INJECTORS WHICH COULD AFFECT FUEL LINES AND LEAD TO LEAKS
Defect: On affected vehicles the damping elements may be missing from some injectors which could affect fuel lines and lead to leaks.
Remedy: Check and install/replace damping elements.  Replace high pressure fuel rail if necessary.

title: 2 SERIES GRAN TOURER
body_text:
Concern: On some diesel vehicles glycol leakage from the EGR-Cooler may occur.  In very rare cases this could lead to the melting of the Intake manifold and in very rare cases result in a possible fire.
Defect: On some diesel vehicles glycol leakage from the EGR-Cooler may occur.  In very rare cases this could lead to the melting of the Intake manifold and in very rare cases result in a possible fire.
Remedy: Initial warning letter with advice to be sent with follow up Letter to call in vehicles for rectification work to be carried out on the affected vehicles once parts are available.  The EGR cooler will be replaced with an optimised component.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBLE REDUCTION IN BRAKE SERVO ASSISTANCE
Defect: It is possible that engine oil can enter the brake servo via the brake vacuum pump and vacuum hose. If this occurs it could damage the brake servo membrane which may leak so causing a reduction in braking assistance and a requirement to apply increased pedal force to achieve the same braking efficiency.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles for the fitment of a modified servo hose. In addition the checking of the brake servo for contamination and replacement if required.

raw_payload.Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles for the fitment of a modified servo hose. In addition the checking of the brake servo for contamination and replacement if required.

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: FRONT SUSPENSION OUTER BALL JOINT MAY BECOME DETACHED
Defect: Under extreme conditions of use the outer ball joint pins may become loose in the front suspension lower arms. Under extreme conditions detachment could occur.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and fit modified lower arms.

title: CULLINAN
body_text:
Concern: THE FUEL TANK FEED LINE CONNECTION MAY NOT BE FITTED CORRECTLY
Defect: The fuel tank feed line connection that is pre-fitted at the supplier to the fuel pump may not be fitted correctly.
Remedy: The fuel tank will need to be lowered and the fuel line feed connection to be checked and refitted as necessary.

title: iX3
body_text:
Concern: IN RARE CASES DUE TO A SOFTWARE ERROR THE FAST INTERMEDIATE CIRCUIT DISCHARGE IS TRIGGERED LATE
Defect: In rare cases due to a software error the fast intermediate circuit discharge is triggered late.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles software in the Battery Management Unit (BMU) will be updated by reprogramming.

raw_payload.Concern: IN RARE CASES DUE TO A SOFTWARE ERROR THE FAST INTERMEDIATE CIRCUIT DISCHARGE IS TRIGGERED LATE

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: THERMAL AGEING OF INLINE FUSE LOCATED ON THE FRONT RIGHT SPRING STRUT DOME
Defect: Thermal ageing of an inline fuse located on the front right spring strut dome.
Remedy: Modify the circuit to a later specification and replace the fuse.

raw_payload.Concern: THERMAL AGEING OF INLINE FUSE LOCATED ON THE FRONT RIGHT SPRING STRUT DOME
raw_payload.Defect: Thermal ageing of an inline fuse located on the front right spring strut dome.
raw_payload.Remedy: Modify the circuit to a later specification and replace the fuse.

title: SPECTRE
body_text:
Concern: Integrated Brake Module
Defect: For the affected vehicle there is an issue involving the integrated brake (IB) system where an unexpected loss of hydraulic brake assist cannot be ruled out
Remedy: The Integrated Brake System will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: Integrated Brake Module
raw_payload.Defect: For the affected vehicle there is an issue involving the integrated brake (IB) system where an unexpected loss of hydraulic brake assist cannot be ruled out
raw_payload.Remedy: The Integrated Brake System will be replaced.

title: 5 SERIES SALOON
body_text:
Concern: THE EXHAUST GAS RECIRCULATION COOLER MAY LEAK OVER TIME AND CAUSE THERMAL INCIDENT
Defect: The Exhaust Gas Recirculation cooler may leak over time and cause thermal incident.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the EGR cooler module will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: THE EXHAUST GAS RECIRCULATION COOLER MAY LEAK OVER TIME AND CAUSE THERMAL INCIDENT
raw_payload.Defect: The Exhaust Gas Recirculation cooler may leak over time and cause thermal incident.
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the EGR cooler module will be replaced.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: BRAKE POWER ASSISTANCE MAY BE AFFECTED
Defect: Brake performance may be affected as a result of oil contamination of the brake servo. The servo membrane could over a period of time soften and fail causing lower levels of servo assistance for the brakes.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the vacuum hose and fit a non-return valve (oil stop) between the booster and servo. Where necessary the servo and master cylinder will be replaced.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: AUXILIARY WATER PUMP'S ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT BOARD COULD LEAD TO A SHORT CIRCUIT
Defect: The auxiliary water pump may short circuit causing a thermal incident.
Remedy: The Auxiliary Water Pump of the affected vehicles will be replaced.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: THE SCREW AT THE BOTTOM STEERING SPINDLE CONNECTION MAY BE INCORRECTLY TIGHTENED
Defect: The screw at the bottom steering spindle connection may be tightened incorrectly.
Remedy: The screw connection at the bottom steering spindle will be checked replaced and tightened to the correct specifications.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: FRONT BRAKE HOSE COULD RUB ON TYRE
Defect: The securing grommet for the front brake hose onto the suspension strut may become detached.  In this situation it is possible that the hose could rub against the inside of the front tyre causing consequential damage to the hose.  In extreme cases brake fluid could leak causing loss of braking action on the front axle.
Remedy: The repair will involve fitting a securing clip to the hose and grommet and checking the hose for damage.

Concern: THE STEERING SHAFT BEARING PLATE MAY NOT BE SCREWED TO THE ENGINE BULKHEAD CORRECTLY
Defect: The steering shaft bearing plate may not have been screwed on correctly.

Concern: THE INTEGRATED BRAKE SYSTEM HYDRAULIC UNIT MAY HAVE BEEN PRODUCED INCORRECTLY

title: 2 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBLE LOSS OF CONTROL
Defect: The bolts securing the rear axle support should not be re-used after removal. Affected vehicles may have had the rear axle support refitted after repair work using the original fitted bolts. These may work and though noise and vibration are likely continued use may affect the vehicle's handling.
Remedy: On affected vehicles replace the rear axle support bolts.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: CHILD SAFETY LOCK MAY NOT ACTIVATE
Defect: It is possible that vehicles fitted with Automatic Soft-Closure Feature (SCA) rear doors may have an issue with out of tolerance on the SCA mechanism which can result in the deactivation of the child locks when they are activated. This could result in a child unintentionally opening the door and exiting the vehicle. The driver is informed that the rear door is open by a warning on the instrument panel and an acoustic (noise) warning.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles to check the locks and replace them if required.

title: i4
body_text:
Concern: During the supplier s production process of the battery cells a manufacturing defect occurred. During assembly of the cell the cathode plate may have been damaged and a broken piece may have entered the interior of the cell.
Defect: During assembly of the HV Battery cell the cathode plate may have been damaged and a broken piece may have entered the interior of the cell.  BMW strongly recommends refraining from charging the high-voltage battery and to not use the vehicles until the repair is performed. BMW also recommends parking the vehicle outdoors and not in the immediate vicinity of buildings.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the defective cell modules of the high-voltage battery will be replaced.

title: Z4
body_text:
Concern: LOSS OF DRIVE
Defect: It is possible that over a period of time the bolt securing the clutch cover/pressure plate may become loose. The drive should be warned of this by the behaviour of the clutch noise from it or inability to select gears. Under certain conditions complete loss of drive could occur which could place the driver and occupant of the vehicle in a prone position.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles and replace the clutch bolts.

title: X6

Concern: DRIVE MAY FAIL
Defect: The power steering pump drive belt tensioner pulley securing bolt may fail which will lead to the loss of power steering assistance and the necessity for the driver to supply additional input to steering the vehicle.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the steering pump drive belt tensioner pulley securing bolt with a more robust version.

Remedy: Check compression springs on the backrest frame (third-row seating) and rework if necessary.

body_text:
Concern: STEERING LINKAGE MAY FOUL A BRAKE PIPE
Defect: There is a possibility of a foul condition between the lower steering linkage heatshield and a high pressure hydraulic pipe that feeds the front brakes on left hand drive naturally aspirated cars.
Remedy: Recall the one affected vehicle and check the subject brake pipe for condition and clearance between it and the heatshield. If necessary the pipe will be repositioned to ensure adequate clearance is achieved. Should the pipe show signs

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: INFLATOR IN DRIVER AIRBAG MODULE MAY NOT BE PRODUCED TO SPECIFICATION
Defect: The driver airbag module may not have been produced to specification. This could lead to excessive pressure building up inside the inflator causing the inflator to rupture resulting in metal fragments passing through the airbag cushion material into the vehicle interior at high speed. This could lead to injury or death of the vehicle occupants.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and replace the driver airbag module.

raw_payload.Concern: INFLATOR IN DRIVER AIRBAG MODULE MAY NOT BE PRODUCED TO SPECIFICATION
raw_payload.Defect: The driver airbag module may not have been produced to specification. This could lead to excessive pressure building up inside the inflator causing the inflator to rupture resulting in metal fragments passing through the airbag cushion material into the vehicle interior at high speed. This could lead to injury or death of the vehicle occupants.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and replace the driver airbag module.

title: 6 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE MAY FAIL
Defect: In individual cases the oil pump may no longer provide the required oil pressure. This may cause severe engine damage. Additionally the occurrence of critical driving situations cannot be ruled out because the oil pressure may drop suddenly while driving.  The driver will usually notice a drop in the engine oil pressure due to the check control message ''Low oil Pressure''. It is also possible the engine block will be damaged and as a result engine oil may be lost.
Remedy: The oil pump will be replaced with the correct version.

raw_payload.Concern: ENGINE MAY FAIL

Additionally the occurrence of critical driving situations cannot be ruled out because the oil pressure may drop suddenly while driving.  The driver will usually notice a drop in the engine oil pressure due to the check control message ''Low oil Pressure''. It is also possible the engine block will be damaged and as a result engine oil may be lost.
raw_payload.Remedy: The oil pump will be replaced with the correct version.

title: M3
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE MAY CUT OUT
Defect: The double clutch is not operating at the optimum level. This could lead to the engine cutting out at low speeds.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to programme the necessary control units.

raw_payload.Concern: ENGINE MAY CUT OUT
raw_payload.Defect: The double clutch is not operating at the optimum level. This could lead to the engine cutting out at low speeds.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to programme the necessary control units.

title: 6 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: RISK OF FIRE
Defect: The positive battery cable routed from the vehicle battery to the engine bay consists of one cable in the luggage compartment and one cable in the underbody.  Both cable are connected by a screw connection and covered by a plastic cover.  In single cases the plastic cover may have been fitted incorrectly during the vehicle production.  Over time an incorrectly fitted cover could cause the screw connections to come loose leading to an increased resistance also humidity may affect the connection and cause high resistance and drop in current.  This can lead to the vehicle not starting and in extreme cases the connection could over heat leading to the possibility of a fire in the luggage area.
Remedy: The clips on the battery will be checked the cover replaced and sealed.

This can lead to the vehicle not starting and in extreme cases the connection could over heat leading to the possibility of a fire in the luggage area.
raw_payload.Remedy: The clips on the battery will be checked the cover replaced and sealed.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: FRONT SEAT BACK-REST HEATER MAY OVERHEAT
Defect: Mechanical loads exerted on the back-rest heating element of the front seats when persons enter or exit the vehicle may result in damage to the door side contact line in the back-rest padding. In the event that this causes a break of a contact line the damaged area may overheat to unacceptable levels with the heater switched on. It would then not be possible to rule out the risk of minor burns to the occupants clothes and possibly their skin.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and check condition where necessary fit an additional in-line fuse.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBLE DETACHMENT OF BALL BEARING FROM FRONT SUSPENSION STRUT TOP MOUNTING
Defect: It is possible that the ball bearing in the front suspension strut top mounting may not have been securely fitted. If the weight of the car is taken off of the road wheel for any reason the bearing may became displaced with a risk of personal injury or damage to the suspension.
Remedy: Replace both front suspension strut mounts.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: RISK OF FIRE
Defect: Moisture may enter the fuel filter heater at an electrical connection. Should this occur it can cause a discharge of the battery causing non starting of the vehicle. Also if it occurs whilst the vehicle is in motion it could result in a fire when the vehicle is left unattended.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles and replace the fuel filter heater.

Concern: THE TANDAM VACUUM/OIL PUMP MAY FAIL
Defect: The fault involves the tandem vacuum/oil pump fitted to the engine. On affected vehicles it is possible that the engine rotates in the opposite direction than it is meant to on start-up causing the tandem pump to fail.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the control unit (DME) will be programmed to prevent the failure from occuring.

Remedy: Replace the airbags on affected vehicles.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: DOOR PRESSURE SENSORS MAY DELAY SIDE AIRBAG DEPLOYMENT FOLLOWING AN IMPACT.
Defect: It has been identified that the connections for the left and right hand door pressure sensors have been reversed at their connection with the airbag control unit. This would have the effect of delaying the detection of a side impact thus causing the airbag to be deployed too late. It should be noted that the fault would not be indicted by the illumination of the airbag system warning lamp.
Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the pin assignment of the airbag control unit corrected.

title: PHANTOM

Concern: SIDE CURTAIN AIRBAGS MAY NOT DEPLOY CORRECTLY
Defect: The side curtain air bags for both left and right sides of the vehicle may not have been produced to specification. It is possible for the side curtain air bag may not position itself as intended during a collision thereby further increasing the risk of injury to the occupants.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and inspect the side curtain air bags on both left and right of the vehicle. Where necessary the side curtain air bag will be replaced.

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: SPARE WHEEL MAY BECOME LOOSE
Defect: The emergency spare wheel mounting may gradually loosen due to vibration. If the flange nut loosens completely the emergency spare wheel well with the spare wheel can suddenly separate from the vehicles underbody.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the flange nut on the mounting plate of the emergency spare wheel well.

title: X5
body_text:

title: i8
body_text:
Concern: STEERING MAY NOT BEHAVE AS DESIGNED
Defect: The engine current sensors have not been calibrated correctly which could lead to an increased recuperation torque during vehicle deceleration. An increased recuperation torque during deceleration could lead to a vehicle oversteer condition in the i3 and vehicle understeer condition in the i8. This could lead to skidding on slippery roads during driving.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and calibrate sensors for measuring the engine current.

title: PARK WARD
body_text:
Concern: AIR CAN ENTER PRIMARY CIRCUIT OF BRAKE MASTER CYLINDER
Defect: The low pressure brake fluid feed pipe between the master cylinder and remote reservoir has the potential to trap air which may in certain circumstances cause partial failure of the primary brake circuit.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace both brake fluid feed hoses and reservoir with a new assembly to a revised specification.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: LOWER STEERING SHAFT MAY FAIL
Defect: Due to the long term effects of corrosion the above vehicles are being recalled. The corrosion is an age related problem and this recall action will target the oldest cars first in groups of 15000 to 20000. It is believed that as long as the vehicle has a current MOT and is serviced correctly any possible corrosion problems with the shaft will be highlighted as a matter of course. If customers are concerned they can visit any BMW dealer to have the shaft checked.
Remedy: DEFECT (cont.) If the component is safe the car will not be repaired at this stage but will be recalled at a later date as originally planned.  REMEDY Recall affected vehicles and fit modified lower steering shaft.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: AIRBAG MAY NOT FUNCTION AS INTENDED
Defect: Due to vibrations particles inside the airbag control unit may cause a short circuit. This may reset the control module  illuminating the airbag warning lamp and prevent the airbag(s) from acting as intended during a collision.
Remedy: On affected vehicle replace the airbag control unit.

raw_payload.Concern: AIRBAG MAY NOT FUNCTION AS INTENDED

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: FIRE MAY OCCUR
Defect: Some engines are fitted with an additional water pump for the turbo charger. In some circumstances a crack may develop which then leads to melting of the plug connections and the housing. Further heat development may then lead to scorch damage. In exceptional cases such overheating can lead to smouldering of the water pump.  If smouldering occurs an engine compartment fire and vehicle fire cannot be excluded.
Remedy: The water pump will be replaced for a new more robust version.

raw_payload.Remedy: The water pump will be replaced for a new more robust version.

Remedy: Check and replace camshaft support housing or complete cylinder head as required.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: DRIVER'S SEAT BELT MAY DE-LATCH
Defect: It has been identified that in the event of a situation which requires activation of the driver's seat belt tensioner. The possibility exists that due to an unfavourable pulse within the seatbelt buckle tensioner mechanism the buckle may open inadvertently.
Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the relevant electronic control units reprogrammed.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: BRAKE LIGHTS MAY FAIL TO OPERATE REMAIN ON OR FLICKER
Defect: There is a possibility that the brake light switch circuits may become defective and cause the brake light circuit to fail stay on continuously or flicker. In extreme cases the brake switch could overheat.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace brake light switch with a modified version.

Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the harness checked and repaired as necessary.

title: 4 SEPIES
body_text:
Concern: FRONT SEAT FIXING MECHANISM MAY BE INSECURE
Defect: Due to a fault in the supplier's production process the bolt on the outer side of the seat mechanism on both front seats might not have been fixed properly. In cases where the bolt loosens the customer would be alerted by noise coming from the seat and a noticeable increase in seat clearance/movement. If this is ignored by the customer the securing bolt on the outer side of the seat might come off. If the securing bolt comes off the seat can move backwards in a rear end crash situation.
Remedy: The bolt on the seat mechanism will be checked for excessive clearance and if necessary replaced.

raw_payload.Remedy: The bolt on the seat mechanism will be checked for excessive clearance and if necessary replaced.

title: Cooper Hatchback 5dr
body_text:
Concern: Integrated Braking System
Defect: On certain vehicles the Integrated Brake (IB) system may be impaired due to contamination between electrical contacts.
Remedy: Vehicles that may be affected by contamination are required to have the vehicle’s software updated which can detect signal interference before it occurs. If symptoms are detected you should refrain from use of the vehicle and the affected Integrated Brake (IB) system component will be replaced.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: Check Windscreen Bonding reworking if necessary
Defect: For affected vehicles there is concern that a gap in the windshield urethane seal at the right A-pillar was created. Due to the gap water can ingress with possible water contamination of the glove box and carpet area including the Body Domain Control unit (BDC) and power distribution box.
Remedy: Check bonding of windshield and for water contamination if necessary reseal windscreen and and inspect electrical components replacing if required.

title: X3
body_text:

title: MINIe Hatchback

Defect: On affected vehicles the seal on the high voltage battery housing may allow moisture to penetrate the battery and allow a fault to develop resulting in a check control message being displayed in some cases the High Voltage battery could switch off even when driving.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the seal will be checked and if required replace any faulty components.

Remedy: On affected vehicles the affected seatbelts will be checked and replaced.

title: 1 SERIES 3 DOOR HATCH
body_text:
Concern: Replace the Driver’s Airbag – Takata PSDI-5
Defect: Affected vehicles may be fitted with a drivers airbag which may deploy with too much explosive force causing sharp metal fragments to be released into the cabin. It has the potential to fatally or seriously injure vehicle occupants.
Remedy: Affected vehicles will have the airbag replaced.

title: i4
body_text:
Concern: Replacing the Cell Module of the High-Voltage Battery
Defect: On affected vehicles the battery module frame may have been manufactured using too much force. In conjunction with swelling force of the battery this could lead to failure and a possible thermal event within the vehicles life time.
Remedy: On affected vehicles the modules will be replaced.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: Replacing the Head Rest (2nd seat row)
Defect: On affected vehicles the outer headrests of the second row of seats may not have been  manufactured correctly. In case of a collision or an accident they may not provide the necessary protection increasing the risk of injury.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the headrest of the second row seats will be  replaced.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: Owners Manual G05
Defect: On affected vehicles in the owner’s manual the maximum permissible limits of the vehicle regarding weight data/additional load values that are specified are stated too high
Remedy: On affected vehicles an updated insert sheet will be added to the owner’s manual.

title: 5 SERIES SALOON
body_text:
Concern: Replace VANOS bolts N51 N52 N55
Defect: On affected vehicles overtime the aluminium screws can loosen or break on the adjustable variable camshaft timing (VANOS) units potentially resulting in engine emergency mode being activated.
Remedy: On affected vehicles the eight aluminium screws will be replaced if evidence of faulty screws is found then the VANOS adjustment unit will also be replaced.

title: GHOST
body_text:
Concern: FRONT AIRBAGS AND BELT TENSIONERS MAY NOT BE TRIGGERED IN A COLLISON
Defect: It has been identified that the airbag/SRS central sensor has been programmed with faulty software. If the vehicle is involved in a frontal or rearward collision then the airbag and seat belt tensioners may not be triggered which could lead to an increased risk of injury to the occupants.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles for the replacement of the airbag/SRS central sensor.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: A MICROPROCESSOR FAULT IN THE AIRBAG CONTROL UNIT CAN LEAD TO FAULTY OPERATING CONDITIONS
Defect: A microprocessor fault in the airbag control unit can lead to faulty operating conditions.
Remedy: The airbag control unit will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: A MICROPROCESSOR FAULT IN THE AIRBAG CONTROL UNIT CAN LEAD TO FAULTY OPERATING CONDITIONS

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: REAR WINDOW MAY DETACH
Defect: It is possible side and rear windows made out of Makrolon (Synthetic Polycarbon Laminate) may become loose due to insufficient adhesion fixing them in place. This will result in a visual and audible warning of the issue to vehicle occupants. If these warnings are ignored it is possible a window could detach from the vehicle.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace the Makrolon windows with conventional glass windows.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: On affected vehicles the bolted connection for the starter-generator may not have been tightened onto the body (ground) according to specifications.
Defect: On affected vehicles the bolted connection for the starter-generator may not have been tightened onto the body (ground) according to specifications.
Remedy: Check and rework the ground connection for the starter generator if required.

raw_payload.Concern: On affected vehicles the bolted connection for the starter-generator may not have been tightened onto the body (ground) according to specifications.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE MAY CUT OUT
Defect: It has been identified that the bolt that connects the ground cable from the wiring harness of the Digital Motor Electronics (DME) to the engine block may not have been tightened/torqued to the correct specification. This can lead to an under voltage supply which can trigger a reset of the DME. This can result in the engine cutting out or depending on vehicle speed to restart/turn on the engine once the reset has been completed.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles to check and if required retorque the bolt for the ground cable on the engine block.

raw_payload.Concern: ENGINE MAY CUT OUT

This can lead to an under voltage supply which can trigger a reset of the DME. This can result in the engine cutting out or depending on vehicle speed to restart/turn on the engine once the reset has been completed.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles to check and if required retorque the bolt for the ground cable on the engine block.

title: MINI 3 Dr Hatch
body_text:
Concern: On some diesel vehicles glycol leakage from the EGR-Cooler may occur.  In very rare cases this could lead to the melting of the Intake manifold and in very rare cases result in a possible fire.
Defect: On some diesel vehicles glycol leakage from the EGR-Cooler may occur.  In very rare cases this could lead to the melting of the Intake manifold and in very rare cases result in a possible fire.
Remedy: Initial warning letter with advice to be sent with follow up Letter to call in vehicles for rectification work to be carried out on the affected vehicles once parts are available.  The EGR cooler will be replaced with an optimised component.

raw_payload.Concern: On some diesel vehicles glycol leakage from the EGR-Cooler may occur.  In very rare cases this could lead to the melting of the Intake manifold and in very rare cases result in a possible fire.

raw_payload.Remedy: Initial warning letter with advice to be sent with follow up Letter to call in vehicles for rectification work to be carried out on the affected vehicles once parts are available.  The EGR cooler will be replaced with an optimised component.

title: X6
body_text:
Concern: POTENTIAL FIRE RISK
Defect: The power steering box housing may not be machined to the correct specification. This could cause an over pressurisation of bellows due to the insecurity of a snap ring and result in a sudden loss of hydraulic pressure. Oil will then be sprayed onto hot engine components and may ignite.
Remedy: Affected vehicles will be recalled to have the steering box replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: POTENTIAL FIRE RISK

This could cause an over pressurisation of bellows due to the insecurity of a snap ring and result in a sudden loss of hydraulic pressure. Oil will then be sprayed onto hot engine components and may ignite.
raw_payload.Remedy: Affected vehicles will be recalled to have the steering box replaced.

title: R52
body_text:
Concern: DIRECTIONAL CONTROL MAY BE LOST
Defect: The front axle inside control arm ball joint may not be torqued to the correct specification which can have a strong negative influence on wheel alignment.  In extreme cases should the screw connections become undone whilst the vehicle is in motion then directional control maybe lost.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles to have the subject connections torqued to the correct specification.

raw_payload.Concern: DIRECTIONAL CONTROL MAY BE LOST

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: BRAKE PEDAL MAY BECOME DETACHED
Defect: The brake pedal mounting shaft may have been incorrectly manufactured.  In this situation the bush may become loose and in very extreme situations the brake pedal can become detached.
Remedy: Check the brake pedal shaft in the bearing  mount and secure it with a new roll pin.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: SOME VEHICLES MAY BE SUSCEPTIBLE TO DAMAGE TO THE ANTI-CORROSION COATING OF THE LOWER STEERING SHAFT
Defect: Some vehicles may be susceptible to damage to the anti-corrosion coating of the lower steering shaft.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the steering shaft must be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: SOME VEHICLES MAY BE SUSCEPTIBLE TO DAMAGE TO THE ANTI-CORROSION COATING OF THE LOWER STEERING SHAFT
raw_payload.Defect: Some vehicles may be susceptible to damage to the anti-corrosion coating of the lower steering shaft.
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the steering shaft must be replaced.

title: M8 GRAN COUPE
body_text:
Concern: INCORRECT ROUTING OF THE GEARBOX WIRING HARNESS
Defect: An incorrect routing of the gearbox wiring harness could lead to wiring harness damage
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the transmission wiring harness will be reworked

raw_payload.Concern: INCORRECT ROUTING OF THE GEARBOX WIRING HARNESS
raw_payload.Defect: An incorrect routing of the gearbox wiring harness could lead to wiring harness damage
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the transmission wiring harness will be reworked

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: PASSENGER SIDE AIRBAG MAY FAIL TO DEPLOY CORRECTLY
Defect: The subject vehicles are equipped with front passenger airbag inflators which could have been assembled with improperly manufactured propellant wafers. Improperly manufactured propellant wafers could cause the inflator to rupture and the front passenger airbag to deploy abnormally in a crash. This could result in increased risk of injury to the occupant and the possibility of a fire.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to inspect and where necessary replace the airbag inflator module.

raw_payload.Concern: PASSENGER SIDE AIRBAG MAY FAIL TO DEPLOY CORRECTLY
raw_payload.Defect: The subject vehicles are equipped with front passenger airbag inflators which could have been assembled with improperly manufactured propellant wafers. Improperly manufactured propellant wafers could cause the inflator to rupture and the front passenger airbag to deploy abnormally in a crash. This could result in increased risk of injury to the occupant and the possibility of a fire.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to inspect and where necessary replace the airbag inflator module.

title: X1
body_text:
Concern: REAR LIGHTS MAY BE FIXED WITH INCORRECT  SECURING NUTS
Defect: The rear lights of affected vehicles might be fixed with securing nuts which may not have been produced to supplier specifications.
Remedy: The rear lights will be checked and if necessary replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: REAR LIGHTS MAY BE FIXED WITH INCORRECT  SECURING NUTS
raw_payload.Defect: The rear lights of affected vehicles might be fixed with securing nuts which may not have been produced to supplier specifications.
raw_payload.Remedy: The rear lights will be checked and if necessary replaced.

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: FRONT SUSPENSION LOWER ARM MAY DETACH
Defect: There is a possibility that under extreme conditions the lower front suspension arm mounting bolts may become loose and the arm may detach.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace front axle support with modified type and longer suspension lower arm bolts.

raw_payload.Concern: FRONT SUSPENSION LOWER ARM MAY DETACH
raw_payload.Defect: There is a possibility that under extreme conditions the lower front suspension arm mounting bolts may become loose and the arm may detach.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and replace front axle support with modified type and longer suspension lower arm bolts.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: BRAKES MAY FAIL
Defect: It has been identified that during the manufacturing process cracks may have formed in the area of the brake disc body. Subsequent surface treatment means that any such crack will not be detectable at a later date. Should a crack have formed this may cause the brake disc to fail whilst the vehicle is in motion causing the affected brake to become inoperative.
Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the rear axle brake discs checked for the affected production date. The brake discs will be checked if necessary.

raw_payload.Concern: BRAKES MAY FAIL
raw_payload.Defect: It has been identified that during the manufacturing process cracks may have formed in the area of the brake disc body. Subsequent surface treatment means that any such crack will not be detectable at a later date. Should a crack have formed this may cause the brake disc to fail whilst the vehicle is in motion causing the affected brake to become inoperative.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the rear axle brake discs checked for the affected production date. The brake discs will be checked if necessary.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: SEAT BELT TENSIONERS
Defect: It is possible that the reinforcement ring in the front seat belt tensioner has not been securely crimped onto the belt tensioner's steel cable. This connection could give in the event of a severe collision and could work loose in extreme cases.
Remedy: Check the production date on both front seat belt tensioners and replace if they fall within the affected production period.

raw_payload.Concern: SEAT BELT TENSIONERS
raw_payload.Defect: It is possible that the reinforcement ring in the front seat belt tensioner has not been securely crimped onto the belt tensioner's steel cable. This connection could give in the event of a severe collision and could work loose in extreme cases.
raw_payload.Remedy: Check the production date on both front seat belt tensioners and replace if they fall within the affected production period.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBILITY OF INADVERTNENT LOSS OF ENGINE POWER
Defect: Due to a hardware fault within the digital engine electronic control unit the possibility exists that the unit could fail completely resulting in loss of engine power.
Remedy: Recall likely to be affected vehicles and replace the control unit with a quality assured item.

raw_payload.Concern: POSSIBILITY OF INADVERTNENT LOSS OF ENGINE POWER
raw_payload.Defect: Due to a hardware fault within the digital engine electronic control unit the possibility exists that the unit could fail completely resulting in loss of engine power.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall likely to be affected vehicles and replace the control unit with a quality assured item.

title: G30 (5-SERIES SALOON)
body_text:
Concern: THE EMERGENCY LOCKING RETRACTOR (ELR) IN THE FRONT SEAT BELT ASSEMBLIES MAY BE FAULTY
Defect: The vehicle sensitive locking mechanism for the front seatbelts may be incorrect causing either the belt not to lock or the webbing very difficult to pull out. If the locking mechanism does not function during a collision it will not restrain the wearer as per design intent and increase the risk of injury.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and check the seat belts on both front seats and if necessary replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: THE EMERGENCY LOCKING RETRACTOR (ELR) IN THE FRONT SEAT BELT ASSEMBLIES MAY BE FAULTY
raw_payload.Defect: The vehicle sensitive locking mechanism for the front seatbelts may be incorrect causing either the belt not to lock or the webbing very difficult to pull out. If the locking mechanism does not function during a collision it will not restrain the wearer as per design intent and increase the risk of injury.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and check the seat belts on both front seats and if necessary replaced.

title: X4
body_text:
Concern: PISTONS IN REAR BRAKE CALIPERS MAY NOT BE COATED TO SPECIFICATIONS
Defect: The fault affects the brake system in particular the brake calipers on the rear axle. Because of a faulty coating hydrogen gas (approximately 10 ml) could be created in the coating of the piston of the brake caliper.  This will be completely outgassed during the first 2 months after the production process causing longer brake pedal travel.  Under certain circumstances the relevant brake pressure cannot be build up in the rear brake circuit. As a result the brake force of the  system may be reduced by up to 30%.  The driver may feel a soft brake pedal and a reduction of the brake performance. In certain cases the rear brake circuit could fail leading to increased risk of an accident.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the brake system will be bled.

raw_payload.Concern: PISTONS IN REAR BRAKE CALIPERS MAY NOT BE COATED TO SPECIFICATIONS
raw_payload.Defect: The fault affects the brake system in particular the brake calipers on the rear axle. Because of a faulty coating hydrogen gas (approximately 10 ml) could be created in the coating of the piston of the brake caliper.  This will be completely outgassed during the first 2 months after the production process causing longer brake pedal travel.  Under certain circumstances the relevant brake pressure cannot be build up in the rear brake circuit. As a result the brake force of the  system may be reduced by up to 30%.  The driver may feel a soft brake pedal and a reduction of the brake performance. In certain cases the rear brake circuit could fail leading to increased risk of an accident.
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the brake system will be bled.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: Replace the Engine Starter Motor
Defect: The Safety Recall relates to the engine starter motor. The fault relates to  the RSW22 starter RWT (reinforced water tightness). It was found that  with RWT starters an increased abrasion of the magnetic contact can  cause the growth of deposits at the lowest point of the relay interior.  This in turn may increase the relay contact resistance resulting in false  starts or even causing short circuits and possibly a thermal reaction.  In case of a short circuit the possibility of a thermal event cannot be excluded. This may happen while attempting to start the vehicle or while  driving due to vibrations causing a short circuit due to moving deposits.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the starter motor will be replaced with a variant  by another supplier with a different design.  Until the repair has been completed it is recommended to not leave the  vehicle running unattended after starting the engine.

This in turn may increase the relay contact resistance resulting in false  starts or even causing short circuits and possibly a thermal reaction.  In case of a short circuit the possibility of a thermal event cannot be excluded. This may happen while attempting to start the vehicle or while  driving due to vibrations causing a short circuit due to moving deposits.
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the starter motor will be replaced with a variant  by another supplier with a different design.  Until the repair has been completed it is recommended to not leave the  vehicle running unattended after starting the engine.

title: MINI PACEMAN
body_text:
Concern: RISK OF FIRE
Defect: In certain conditions moisture could gain entry into the wiring connector that powers the Electronic Power Steering (EPS) and the EPS unit. This moisture can then in very rare cases penetrate the EPS through the ground pin and cause overheating of the ground connector pin  which can in turn lead to fire.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the supplementary wiring harness with one that has an improved connector sealing arrangement.

raw_payload.Concern: THE PRINTED CIRCUIT BOARD WAS NOT MANUFACTURED ACCORDING TO SPECIFICATIONS
raw_payload.Defect: The electric motor electronics are not manufactured to specification.
raw_payload.Remedy: The electric motor electronics EME of vehicles affected will be replaced.

title: 6 SERIES CONVERTIBLE
body_text:
Concern: PEDESTRIAN SAFETY SYSTEM MAY NOT PERFORM CORRECTLY
Defect: The pedestrian safety function in the bonnet may fail to trigger.
Remedy: Replace the pedestrian safety sensor system.

raw_payload.Concern: PEDESTRIAN SAFETY SYSTEM MAY NOT PERFORM CORRECTLY
raw_payload.Defect: The pedestrian safety function in the bonnet may fail to trigger.
raw_payload.Remedy: Replace the pedestrian safety sensor system.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: STEERING RACK MIGHT LOCK
Defect: If the retaining ring inside the steering gear has not been fitted correctly the sealing cap would not be fixed axially and might start moving. If the sealing cap has moved the ball return channel would not be fixed anymore and might move out of position  possibly resulting in a ball jam accompanied by an extensively increased steering force and possibly lock.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to fit a new steering rack and ensure it is programmed correctly.

If the sealing cap has moved the ball return channel would not be fixed anymore and might move out of position  possibly resulting in a ball jam accompanied by an extensively increased steering force and possibly lock.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to fit a new steering rack and ensure it is programmed correctly.

title: X6
body_text:
Concern: THE ISOFIX CHILD RESTRAINT U-LOOP MAY BREAK OFF THE CARRIER PLATE
Defect: The ISOFIX child restraint U-Loop may break off the carrier plate resulting in the child seat no longer being secure.
Remedy: Weld an additional metal angle bracket to each ISOFIX U-Loop

raw_payload.Concern: THE ISOFIX CHILD RESTRAINT U-LOOP MAY BREAK OFF THE CARRIER PLATE
raw_payload.Defect: The ISOFIX child restraint U-Loop may break off the carrier plate resulting in the child seat no longer being secure.
raw_payload.Remedy: Weld an additional metal angle bracket to each ISOFIX U-Loop

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: BRAKE FLUID MAY LEAK
Defect: If a brake fluid leak occurs the brake fluid low level warning may be given too late.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to check the serial number of the brake servo and where necessary replace the cap for the expansion tank which includes the warning switch.

raw_payload.Concern: BRAKE FLUID MAY LEAK
raw_payload.Defect: If a brake fluid leak occurs the brake fluid low level warning may be given too late.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: STEERING LOCK MAY INADVERTENTLY ENGAGE
Defect: The control cam of the steering lock may break. This is most likely to occur as the vehicle is started or parked. However the possibility of this occurring whilst the vehicle is in motion cannot be ruled out. In this event the steering lock could engage with the effect that it would no longer be possible to steer the vehicle.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles check manufacturer of lock and if necessary replace steering lock.

However the possibility of this occurring whilst the vehicle is in motion cannot be ruled out. In this event the steering lock could engage with the effect that it would no longer be possible to steer the vehicle.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall affected vehicles check manufacturer of lock and if necessary replace steering lock.

title: X7
body_text:
Concern: INCORRECT HEADREST RODS MAY HAVE BEEN INSTALLED IN THE BACKREST OF THE SECOND ROW OF SEATS ON THE OUTER SIDE
Defect: Incorrect headrest rods may have been installed in the backrest of the second row of seats on the outer side.
Remedy: On affected vehicles the outer headrest of the second seat row will be checked and replaced if necessary.

raw_payload.Concern: INCORRECT HEADREST RODS MAY HAVE BEEN INSTALLED IN THE BACKREST OF THE SECOND ROW OF SEATS ON THE OUTER SIDE
raw_payload.Defect: Incorrect headrest rods may have been installed in the backrest of the second row of seats on the outer side.
raw_payload.Remedy: On affected vehicles the outer headrest of the second seat row will be checked and replaced if necessary.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: SECURITY OF STUB AXLE MOUNTINGS
Defect: The three bolts which secure the stub axle to the spring strut may not have been fully torqued on initial assembly.
Remedy: Recall the affected vehicles and check the torque setting on the bolts involved retorqueing as required. Any bolts found to have been loose (as distinct from being insufficiently torqued) will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: SECURITY OF STUB AXLE MOUNTINGS

title: M3
body_text:
Concern: IT IS POSSIBLE A SCREW OF  MECHATRONICS TRANSMISSION CONTROL MAY BE MISSING
Defect: It is possible that a screw of mechatronics transmission control may be missing.
Remedy: The mechatronics unit will be replaced inside the transmission

raw_payload.Concern: IT IS POSSIBLE A SCREW OF  MECHATRONICS TRANSMISSION CONTROL MAY BE MISSING
raw_payload.Defect: It is possible that a screw of mechatronics transmission control may be missing.
raw_payload.Remedy: The mechatronics unit will be replaced inside the transmission

title: I01
body_text:
Concern: VENTILATION LINE MAY RUB AGAINST POSITIVE BATTERY CABLE
Defect: Due to inappropriate routing the ventilation line might rub against the battery positive cable which can cause a leakage over time. If leakage occurs fuel vapours will enter the engine compartment causing the risk of fire.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and check the fuel tank ventilation line. If damaged the ventilation line will be replaced. In all cases an additional clip will be attached between the tank ventilation line and the battery positive cable.

raw_payload.Concern: VENTILATION LINE MAY RUB AGAINST POSITIVE BATTERY CABLE
raw_payload.Defect: Due to inappropriate routing the ventilation line might rub against the battery positive cable which can cause a leakage over time. If leakage occurs fuel vapours will enter the engine compartment causing the risk of fire.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and check the fuel tank ventilation line. If damaged the ventilation line will be replaced. In all cases an additional clip will be attached between the tank ventilation line and the battery positive cable.

title: 2 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: FRONT AXLE SWIVEL BEARINGS MAY NOT HAVE BEEN PRODUCED TO SPECIFICATION
Defect: Due to a fault in the supplier production process the swivel bearings on the front axle of the affected vehicles may not have been produced to specification. Due to an insufficient heat treatment the fracture strain has been reduced.  The affected swivel bearings could break causing loss of wheel guidance during driving.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles both swivel bearings will be checked and if necessary replaced with a part produced to specification.

raw_payload.Concern: FRONT AXLE SWIVEL BEARINGS MAY NOT HAVE BEEN PRODUCED TO SPECIFICATION
raw_payload.Defect: Due to a fault in the supplier production process the swivel bearings on the front axle of the affected vehicles may not have been produced to specification. Due to an insufficient heat treatment the fracture strain has been reduced.  The affected swivel bearings could break causing loss of wheel guidance during driving.
raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles both swivel bearings will be checked and if necessary replaced with a part produced to specification.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBILITY THAT GEARBOX MAY LOCK UP
Defect: Possibility that the automatic transmission may lock up at speeds below 15mph.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and fit a new quality assured gearbox.

raw_payload.Concern: POSSIBILITY THAT GEARBOX MAY LOCK UP
raw_payload.Defect: Possibility that the automatic transmission may lock up at speeds below 15mph.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and fit a new quality assured gearbox.

title: VARIOUS MODELS
body_text:
Concern: BRAKING ASSISTANCE MAY BE AFFECTED
Defect: It is possible for the lubrication to the vacuum pump to be interrupted because of a nonconformity with the sealing cap fitted to the intake camshaft. The sealing cap may move and prevent lubrication reaching the vacuum pump causing it over a period to seize. The driver will probably note increasing input is necessary to stop the vehicle. Should the vacuum pump fail completely the vehicle can still be braked albeit with increased pressure applied to the foot brake control.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to check the sealing cap of the intake camshaft. If necessary the sealing cap in the camshaft will be secured with a spacer.

raw_payload.Concern: BRAKING ASSISTANCE MAY BE AFFECTED
raw_payload.Defect: It is possible for the lubrication to the vacuum pump to be interrupted because of a nonconformity with the sealing cap fitted to the intake camshaft. The sealing cap may move and prevent lubrication reaching the vacuum pump causing it over a period to seize. The driver will probably note increasing input is necessary to stop the vehicle. Should the vacuum pump fail completely the vehicle can still be braked albeit with increased pressure applied to the foot brake control.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to check the sealing cap of the intake camshaft. If necessary the sealing cap in the camshaft will be secured with a spacer.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBLE FLUID LEAK FROM POWER STEERING
Defect: The threaded unions on the pressure lines for the power steering gear may not have been sufficiently tightened and could leak oil. Leaking oil could cause the power steering system to fail and in extreme cases escaping oil could ignite on hot engine components.
Remedy: Check union bolts for signs of leakage. If leaks are found at either of the unions the steering gear will be replaced. If no leaks are found the union bolts will be checked for correct torque.

raw_payload.Concern: POSSIBLE FLUID LEAK FROM POWER STEERING
raw_payload.Defect: The threaded unions on the pressure lines for the power steering gear may not have been sufficiently tightened and could leak oil. Leaking oil could cause the power steering system to fail and in extreme cases escaping oil could ignite on hot engine components.
raw_payload.Remedy: Check union bolts for signs of leakage. If leaks are found at either of the unions the steering gear will be replaced. If no leaks are found the union bolts will be checked for correct torque.

title: GHOST
body_text:
Concern: SHORT CIRCUIT MAY OCCUR
Defect: Due to the ingress of moisture it is possible that a short circuit can occur in the additional coolant pump for the exhaust turbocharger. The driver will be informed of the issue by a warning message on the display in the instrument panel.  If not addressed it is possible for further heat build up in the pump which may cause the plug connection and housing to melt and in extreme cases a localised fire in the engine compartment or vehicle.
Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles for the replacement of the additional coolant pump.

raw_payload.Concern: SHORT CIRCUIT MAY OCCUR
raw_payload.Defect: Due to the ingress of moisture it is possible that a short circuit can occur in the additional coolant pump for the exhaust turbocharger. The driver will be informed of the issue by a warning message on the display in the instrument panel.  If not addressed it is possible for further heat build up in the pump which may cause the plug connection and housing to melt and in extreme cases a localised fire in the engine compartment or vehicle.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall all affected vehicles for the replacement of the additional coolant pump.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: OIL COOLER MAY LEAK
Defect: The engine oil cooler feed pipe may disconnect and leak oil. This may reach the front brakes and affect the braking efficiency.
Remedy: On affected vehicles replace engine cooler pipe.

raw_payload.Concern: OIL COOLER MAY LEAK
raw_payload.Defect: The engine oil cooler feed pipe may disconnect and leak oil. This may reach the front brakes and affect the braking efficiency.
raw_payload.Remedy: On affected vehicles replace engine cooler pipe.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: REAR SPOILER TO BOOT LID MAY NOT BE ATTACHED SECURELY
Defect: Vehicles may not have had the rear spoiler properly attached
Remedy: The screw connection of the rear spoiler will be inspected and if necessary screws installed.

raw_payload.Concern: REAR SPOILER TO BOOT LID MAY NOT BE ATTACHED SECURELY
raw_payload.Defect: Vehicles may not have had the rear spoiler properly attached
raw_payload.Remedy: The screw connection of the rear spoiler will be inspected and if necessary screws installed.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: FRONT PASSENGER AIRBAG MAY NOT DEPLOY CORRECTLY
Defect: The defect concerns the front passenger air bag. The skin of certain instrument panels was not correctly scored. In case of accident with air bag deployment the predetermined breaking point will not tear open resulting in the air bag deploying in the instrument panel. This will lead to insufficient restraint effect and possibly release of parts of the air bag system into the passenger compartment.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the instrument panel.

raw_payload.Concern: FRONT PASSENGER AIRBAG MAY NOT DEPLOY CORRECTLY

In case of accident with air bag deployment the predetermined breaking point will not tear open resulting in the air bag deploying in the instrument panel. This will lead to insufficient restraint effect and possibly release of parts of the air bag system into the passenger compartment.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the instrument panel.

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: STATIC ELECTRICITY MAY ACCUMULATE IN THE FUEL FILLER TUBE
Defect: Static electricity may accumulate in the fuel filler tube.
Remedy: Fit a modified fuel filler tube assembly incorporating a ground wire.

raw_payload.Concern: STATIC ELECTRICITY MAY ACCUMULATE IN THE FUEL FILLER TUBE
raw_payload.Defect: Static electricity may accumulate in the fuel filler tube.
raw_payload.Remedy: Fit a modified fuel filler tube assembly incorporating a ground wire.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBLE UNSCHEDULED DEPLOYMENT OF SIDE AIRBAGS AND ACTIVATION OF BATTERY ISOLATOR SWITCH
Defect: In certain driving conditions it is possible for the side airbags and the battery terminal (safety isolation switch) to trigger when this is not necessary ie when no accident has occurred.
Remedy: Recall the affected vehicles and recode the airbag control unit.

raw_payload.Concern: POSSIBLE UNSCHEDULED DEPLOYMENT OF SIDE AIRBAGS AND ACTIVATION OF BATTERY ISOLATOR SWITCH
raw_payload.Defect: In certain driving conditions it is possible for the side airbags and the battery terminal (safety isolation switch) to trigger when this is not necessary ie when no accident has occurred.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the affected vehicles and recode the airbag control unit.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: EXCESSIVE INCREASE OF COOLING SYSTEM TEMPERATURE COULD LEAD TO A BURSTING OF THE HEATER MATRIX RADIATOR
Defect: Excessive increase of cooling system temperature could (in a worst case) lead to a bursting of the heater matrix radiator.
Remedy: Fitting an additional thermostat and modified hoses into the heating system to bypass the heat exchanger if overheating was to occur.

raw_payload.Concern: EXCESSIVE INCREASE OF COOLING SYSTEM TEMPERATURE COULD LEAD TO A BURSTING OF THE HEATER MATRIX RADIATOR
raw_payload.Defect: Excessive increase of cooling system temperature could (in a worst case) lead to a bursting of the heater matrix radiator.
raw_payload.Remedy: Fitting an additional thermostat and modified hoses into the heating system to bypass the heat exchanger if overheating was to occur.

title: 1 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: FIRE MAY OCCUR
Defect: The top seat belt automatic reel with pyrotechnic ignited pre-tensioner fitted is mounted too close to the sound insulation both nearside and offside. If the vehicle is involved in a substantial collision where the pre-tensioners are deployed a fire may occur as it is possible for the hot gases to ignite the sound insulation.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to rework the sound insulation fitted to both nearside and offside and if necessary fit new top seat belts

raw_payload.Concern: FIRE MAY OCCUR
raw_payload.Defect: The top seat belt automatic reel with pyrotechnic ignited pre-tensioner fitted is mounted too close to the sound insulation both nearside and offside. If the vehicle is involved in a substantial collision where the pre-tensioners are deployed a fire may occur as it is possible for the hot gases to ignite the sound insulation.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to rework the sound insulation fitted to both nearside and offside and if necessary fit new top seat belts

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: A STARTER MOTOR BOLT MAY HAVE BEEN INSTALLED INCORRECTLY AND BECOME LOST INSIDE THE TRANSMISSION BELL HOUSING
Defect: A starter bolt may have been installed incorrectly and become lost inside the transmission bell housing.
Remedy: The starter motor will be removed and checked for a bolt that is adrift any damage found will be rectified.

raw_payload.Concern: A STARTER MOTOR BOLT MAY HAVE BEEN INSTALLED INCORRECTLY AND BECOME LOST INSIDE THE TRANSMISSION BELL HOUSING
raw_payload.Defect: A starter bolt may have been installed incorrectly and become lost inside the transmission bell housing.
raw_payload.Remedy: The starter motor will be removed and checked for a bolt that is adrift any damage found will be rectified.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: VARIATION TO ELECTRICAL RESISTANCE IN THE BLOWER REGULATOR WIRING HARNESS CONNECTOR
Defect: Over time irregularities in the crimp connection in conjunction with relative movements of the tin coated connector at the end of the wiring harness which connects to the tin coated blower regulator pin could lead to frictional corrosion. In combination with very high current flow which would occur which could lead to a temperature increase. As a result the blower regulator wiring could be damaged and could lead to partial contact of individual wire strands. this could result in an irregular current flow further overheating and increase the risk of fire.
Remedy: The blower regulator wiring harness will be inspected and a new part will be installed.

raw_payload.Remedy: The blower regulator wiring harness will be inspected and a new part will be installed.

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: The high-pressure fuel rail may not have been produced according to specifications
Defect: The high-pressure fuel rail may not have been produced according to specifications.
Remedy: Replace the high-pressure fuel rail.

raw_payload.Concern: The high-pressure fuel rail may not have been produced according to specifications
raw_payload.Defect: The high-pressure fuel rail may not have been produced according to specifications.
raw_payload.Remedy: Replace the high-pressure fuel rail.

title: 6 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: REAR SHOCK ABSORBER MOUNTING MAY FAIL
Defect: It has been identified that the lower rubber mounting on the metal bush of the rear shock absorbers may fail to meet the quality requirement. Dependant on the mileage and operating conditions the rubber mounting may come completely free from the shock absorber. If this occurs the bodywork would no longer be supported above the wheels causing it to come into contact with the wheel concerned. Should this occur the possibility exists that a critical driving condition might occur or even the risk of an accident.
Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the rear shock absorbers checked and replaced if necessary.

raw_payload.Concern: REAR SHOCK ABSORBER MOUNTING MAY FAIL

Should this occur the possibility exists that a critical driving condition might occur or even the risk of an accident.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recalled vehicles will have the rear shock absorbers checked and replaced if necessary.

title: F90
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE CONTROL UNIT  CONTROL OF FUEL PUMP
Defect: Insufficient control of the fuel pump by the engine control unit the fuel pump may stop functioning in certain driving conditions which could lead in individual cases to a drop of the fuel pressure.  When the fuel pressure drops an engine stalling cannot be excluded.
Remedy: On affected vehicles the engine control unit will be reprogrammed

title: i3
body_text:
Concern: THE CONTROL RING OF THE BACKREST RECLINER IN THE SECOND SEAT ROW MAY BREAK
Defect: The control ring of the recliner of the backrest in the second seat row may break due to insufficient material performance.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the seat frames of the second seat row will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: THE CONTROL RING OF THE BACKREST RECLINER IN THE SECOND SEAT ROW MAY BREAK

raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the seat frames of the second seat row will be replaced.

title: SILVER SERIES
body_text:
Concern: HEATED SEATS MAY REACH AN UNACCEPTABLY HIGH TEMPERATURE
Defect: On the vehicles affected the potential exists for the surface of each heated seat to reach an unacceptably high temperature. In extreme cases this could cause damage to anything in contact with the seat cushion.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and fit an additional control unit to each heated seat.

raw_payload.Concern: HEATED SEATS MAY REACH AN UNACCEPTABLY HIGH TEMPERATURE

title: 3 SERIES SALOON
body_text:
Concern: MANUFACTURING DEFECT WITH WINDING OF CELL-INTERNAL FILMS FOR HIGH VOLTAGE BATTERY
Defect: The safety recall relates to a problem with a cell module within the high-voltage battery.
Remedy: Replace the affected high-voltage cell module.

raw_payload.Concern: MANUFACTURING DEFECT WITH WINDING OF CELL-INTERNAL FILMS FOR HIGH VOLTAGE BATTERY
raw_payload.Defect: The safety recall relates to a problem with a cell module within the high-voltage battery.

title: 3 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: CORROSION ON LOWER STEERING SHAFT
Defect: Instances of corrosion on the corrugated section of the lower steering shaft have occurred. This could lead to eventual weakening of the shaft.
Remedy: All vehicles fitted with corrugated type lower steering shafts will have them replaced with quality assured examples.

raw_payload.Concern: CORROSION ON LOWER STEERING SHAFT

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: THE STEERING COLUMN  SHAFT COULD DETACH FROM THE DOUBLE JOINT
Defect: The steering column shaft assembly may detach from the bottom double joint.
Remedy: Check the bottom end of the steering shaft and if required resecure the shaft using a new screw.

raw_payload.Concern: THE STEERING COLUMN  SHAFT COULD DETACH FROM THE DOUBLE JOINT

title: MINI
body_text:
Concern: DIRECTIONAL CONTROL COULD BE LOST
Defect: The Anti - Roll Bar fitted to the rear axle has too large a diameter. Detachment may occur with loss of directional control
Remedy: Recall affected vehicle and replace Anti - Roll  Bar.

raw_payload.Concern: DIRECTIONAL CONTROL COULD BE LOST

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: ELECTRIC FUEL PUMP MAY FAIL
Defect: Due to a manufacturing defect which causes premature wear of the fuel pump commutator it is possible that the engine might cut out whilst driving.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the fuel pump.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: TYRES MAY HAVE CUTS IN SIDE WALLS.
Defect: Cuts have been observed on isolated Continental tyres fitted to vehicles. The cuts (1-5 cm in length) are of varying severity and can lead to a loss of tyre pressure. This can destroy the tyre if the vehicle continues to be driven.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles examine all tyres and replace any found with cuts in the sidewalls.

raw_payload.Concern: TYRES MAY HAVE CUTS IN SIDE WALLS.

raw_payload.Remedy: Recall affected vehicles examine all tyres and replace any found with cuts in the sidewalls.

title: 7 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE MAY CUT OUT WHILST THE CAR IS BEING DRIVEN
Defect: Due to an ECU software error within the electrical system the fuel pump may operate at an insufficient speed once the fuel tank level has been reduced to less than 30 litres.
Remedy: Likely to be affected vehicles will be recalled to have the ECU's reprogrammed with updated software.

body_text:
Concern: On affected vehicles the High voltage battery may suffer a low impedance short circuit within a cell module

Defect: The front seat occupancy detector can fail to detect that a seat is occupied with the result that the front and associated side air bags and seat belt pre-tensioners may fail to operate in the event of a collision.
Remedy: Recall likely to be affected vehicles and carry out full coding of the ECUs reprogramming as necessary.

title: i7xDrive

Concern: On affected vehicles under certain conditions due to an increased loading on the wiper arm it may result in the loss of movement of the wipers
Defect: On affected vehicles under certain conditions due to an increased loading on the wiper arm it may result in the loss of movement of the wipers.
Remedy: Inspect the wiper mechanism on affected vehicles and replace if necessary.

title: 6 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: REAR TYRE MAY HAVE SUDDEN LOSS OF TYRE PRESSURE
Defect: There is a possibility that the tyres on the affected vehicles may have been damaged when they were fitted to the rim. If the tyres have been damaged this may lead to a sudden loss of pressure in the rear tyres.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and replace all the tyres

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: ENGINE COOLING FAN MAY FAIL.
Defect: Manufacturing faults and software errors in the electronics of the electric fan could cause these components to be thermally overloaded and destroyed. In isolated cases the overloading could lead to scorching or a fire in the fan.
Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and check the fan type and build. Replace the fan where necessary.

raw_payload.Remedy: Recall affected vehicles and check the fan type and build. Replace the fan where necessary.

title: GHOST
body_text:
Concern: THE AUXILLIARY WATER PUMP'S ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT BOARD CAN LEAD TO SHORT CIRCUIT
Defect: The auxiliary water pump may short circuit causing a thermal incident.
Remedy: The Auxiliary Water Pump of the affected vehicles will be replaced.

raw_payload.Concern: THE AUXILLIARY WATER PUMP'S ELECTRONIC CIRCUIT BOARD CAN LEAD TO SHORT CIRCUIT
raw_payload.Defect: The auxiliary water pump may short circuit causing a thermal incident.
raw_payload.Remedy: The Auxiliary Water Pump of the affected vehicles will be replaced.

title: M5
body_text:
Concern: DISTANCE BETWEEN SENSOR AND IN-TANK FUEL LINE COULD BE TOO SMALL
Defect: The fault affects the fuel level sensor within the fuel tank.The distance between the sensor and an in-tank fuel line could be too small which could allow the sensor to contact the fuel line and in some cases become stuck.  If the fuel sensor becomes stuck the fuel gauge in the instrument cluster displays a larger amount of fuel than is actually in the tank. Therefore the tank could become empty leading to the engine stalling without prior warning.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the fuel line supply unit system will be reworked.

raw_payload.Remedy: On the affected vehicles the fuel line supply unit system will be reworked.

title: 2 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: THE ROOF LINING STIFFENING PLATES MAY NOT BE BONDED CORRECTLY
Defect: The roof lining stiffening plates may not be bonded correctly.
Remedy: The roof headliner will be inspected and reworked or replaced if required.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: THE REINFORCEMENT RING (THIMBLE) MAY NOT HAVE BEEN SECURELY CRIMPED ONTO THE BELT TENSIONER'S STEEL CABLE
Defect: The reinforcement ring (thimble) may not have been securely crimped onto the belt tensioner's steel cable.
Remedy: Check the belt buckle and if necessary conduct replacement of the lower strap including belt tensioner.

title: iX1
body_text:
Concern: Front driveshaft may come loose from the eDrive motor under certain circumstances.
Defect: Front driveshaft may come loose from the eDrive motor under certain circumstances.
Remedy: Replace the affected driveshaft/s

title: MINI John Cooper Works
body_text:
Concern: BRAKE PERFORMANCE COULD BE AFFECTED
Defect: It is possible that the vehicle has been fitted with 16 inch front brake discs instead of the required 17 inch brake discs.  If this is the case extreme braking could result in the over heating of the brake system and introduce vapour (air) bubbles into the brake fluid.  This could affect the braking performance.
Remedy: Recall all potentially affected vehicles and check the size of the brakes discs on the front axle.  If 16 inch brake discs are found replace these with the correct 17 inch brakes discs and brake pads.

raw_payload.Concern: BRAKE PERFORMANCE COULD BE AFFECTED

If this is the case extreme braking could result in the over heating of the brake system and introduce vapour (air) bubbles into the brake fluid.  This could affect the braking performance.
raw_payload.Remedy: Recall all potentially affected vehicles and check the size of the brakes discs on the front axle.  If 16 inch brake discs are found replace these with the correct 17 inch brakes discs and brake pads.

title: E53
body_text:
Concern: AIRBAG CONTROL UNIT FAULT DUE TO INADEQUATE CODING DATA WHICH COULD LEAD TO ACTIVATION
Defect: Possible unintentional activation of the front airbags due to inadequate coding data.
Remedy: On the affected vehicles the airbag control unit will be encoded.

title: M3
body_text:
Concern: REAR WINDOW PANE MAY DETACH
Defect: The rear side windows and the rear windscreen all made from a synthetic polycarbonate may detach from the vehicle causing a danger to other road users and possibly pedestrians.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to replace the rear side windows and if necessary the rear windscreen with conventional glass windows.

title: X7
body_text:
Concern: AN INCORRECT INSTALLATION OF THE HEAD AIRBAGS ON DRIVER AND PASSENGER SIDE WOULD RESULT IN AN INCOMPLETE INFLATION
Defect: Driver and passenger side head airbags may have been twisted on installation
Remedy: The head airbags on the driver and passenger side will be checked and if necessary reworked

title: i3
body_text:

Remedy: The defective cell module will be replaced.

title: 5 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: POSSIBILITY OF INABILITY TO ENGAGE GEAR OR DECLUTCH
Defect: Possibility that the pin operating the master cylinder may have been welded incorrectly. Resulting in the inability (on failure) to de-clutch or engage a gear.
Remedy: Recall likely to be affected vehicles inspect the subject weld and replace the clutch pedal if the weld is found to be faulty.

title: X3
body_text:
Concern: ON AFFECTED VEHICLES SEAT BELT MOUNTING BOLTS MAY NOT BE SECURELY ATTACHED
Defect: On affected vehicles seat belt mounting bolts may not be securely attached.
Remedy: Check connections of both front seat belts and rework if necessary.

title: i4
body_text:
Concern: CRASH PADS MAY NOT HAVE BEEN INSTALLED ON THE HIGH VOLTAGE BATTERY DURING VEHICLE ASSEMBLY
Defect: Crash pads may not have been installed on the high-voltage battery during vehicle assembly.
Remedy: Vehicles will be inspected and if necessary the crash pads will be retrofitted.

title: 1 SERIES
body_text:
Concern: BACKREST COULD FOLD FORWARD UNDER HEAVY LOAD
Defect: The latching element of the Easy Entry Kit on the driver's seat does not lock correctly into the lower swinging arm. As a result the backrest could fold under heavy load such as in a collision or unsecured load in the rear and harsh braking.
Remedy: The vehicle that is likely to be affected will be recalled and a new seat mechanism fitted. R/2018/027 refers.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: AIRBAG MAY DEPLOY INADVERTENTLY
Defect: The front air bags may trigger unnecessarily and the possibility of occupant injury cannot be excluded.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected to recode the air bag control unit.

title: X5
body_text:
Concern: WHEEL BOLTS ON LEFT SIDE MAY NOT HAVE BEEN TIGHTENED CORRECTLY
Defect: The wheel bolts (left side) that may not have been tightened to specifications
Remedy: On the affected vehicles all wheel bolts on the left side will be replaced and tightened to specifications.

title: X1
body_text:
Concern: FRONT PASSENGER AIRBAG MAYNOT  DEPLOY CORRECTLY
Defect: It is possible a recess on the inside of the instrument panel was not machined correctly. The recess is the predetermined break point of the instrument panel when the front passenger airbag is activated. If the milling has not been carried out correctly the proper deployment of the front passenger airbag cannot be ensured.
Remedy: Recall the vehicles that are likely to be affected and check the milling of the predetermined break point. Where necessary the instrument panel will be replaced.
